verdict.rationale: Hard gate triggered: the project's rural set-aside could not be reproduced and appears invalid, and a contradicted material claim about job creation was confirmed by a second source. Both scores are high and weakly evidenced.

factors.name: Regional Center good standing
factors.summary: Found on a list but with a prior compliance notice.

factors.name: I-956F project approval status
factors.summary: I-956F reportedly filed; status not independently confirmed.

factors.name: Job-creation methodology & cushion
factors.summary: Claimed cushion contradicted; jobs rely on aggressive expenditure assumptions.

factors.name: TEA / set-aside qualification validity
factors.summary: Rural set-aside not reproducible — location sits inside an MSA per Census data.

factors.name: RIA compliance: fund admin, audits, securities
Capital-stack position & EB-5 %
factors.summary: EB-5 appears to be ~70% of the stack and the de-facto equity / first-loss tranche.

factors.name: Loan vs equity model & terms
factors.summary: Subordinated equity with no defined exit.

factors.name: Collateral & guarantees
factors.summary: No recorded collateral; guarantor is an affiliated shell.

factors.name: Developer track record & balance sheet
factors.summary: Developer linked to a prior project default in court records.

factors.name: Escrow & redeployment (financial)
factors.summary: Funds released to sponsor on subscription.

factors.name: Fees & conflicts of interest
factors.summary: Heavy affiliate fee stacking and related-party lease.

factors.name: Return-of-capital / distribution history
factors.summary: No evidence of prior capital returns.

claims.claim_text: The project qualifies for the rural set-aside.
claims.note: Census data places the site within a Metropolitan Statistical Area; the rural claim is invalid.
Independent recompute of the stated spend and multipliers yields well under 10 jobs/investor; confirmed by a second analyst source.

claims.claim_text: EB-5 is a minority slice of the capital stack.
claims.note: Public financing summary suggests EB-5 is the dominant (~70%) first-loss tranche.

claims.claim_text: The developer has a flawless completion record.
claims.note: A federal docket shows a prior project default/receivership involving the principal.

red_flags.title: Rural set-aside invalid
red_flags.detail: The site is within an MSA per Census data; the rural reserved-category basis for the $800K amount fails.

red_flags.title: Job-creation shortfall (confirmed)
red_flags.detail: Independent recompute and a second source show fewer than 10 jobs/investor.

red_flags.title: Developer prior default
red_flags.detail: Federal court records show a prior project receivership involving the principal.

red_flags.title: EB-5 is the first-loss tranche
red_flags.detail: EB-5 appears to make up ~70% of the capital stack with no senior protection.
red_flags.issuer_framing: Website implies EB-5 is a minority slice.

data_gaps.why: No USCIS notice or receipt located.

data_gaps.closes_with: An engagement letter from an independent fund administrator.

checklist.text: Regional center active and not terminated.
Reserved-category set-aside independently confirmed and valid.
Job creation provides a meaningful cushion above 10/investor.
Developer track record checked; no undisclosed defaults.
Engage your own independent immigration attorney and financial advisor.
